title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Defense · Operations Transfer
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer local clusters, cluster lifecycle, conformance, upgrades, backup, and production defense into a new cluster lifecycle and recovery defense. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cluster lifecycle and recovery defense with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.
Trace kubeadm bootstrap, certificates, static Pods, control-plane endpoints, etcd membership, kubelet configuration, CNI installation, join tokens, and high-availability responsibilities.
Evaluate Kubernetes conformance, supported versions, distribution lifecycle, cloud integration, add-ons, feature gates, APIs, operational ownership, and vendor support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer, keep K8s Minikube Local Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Minikube Local Transfer condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes 1.36 Releases, Version Skew, and API Reference — Controls supported releases, patch lifecycle, component skew, API groups, schemas, feature states, deprecations, downloads, and upgrade compatibility.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Minikube Local Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.

For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Minikube Local Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A successful minikube workload proves multi-node production networking, storage, load balancing, and failure recovery.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
State the K8s Minikube Local Transfer preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A successful minikube workload proves multi-node production networking, storage, load balancing, and failure recovery. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-19gt5r". Tailor its cluster-recovery-defense.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, keep K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone-19gt5r
  - competency: k8s-disaster-recovery-capstone
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: disaster recovery capstone
    environment: remote context with short-lived federated workload identity
    fault-injection: drain a node while the disruption budget has no headroom
    retained-proof: scheduler event eligible domains skew and placement result
    case-key: 19gt5r
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Test an acceptance boundary · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes 1.36 Releases, Version Skew, and API Reference — Controls supported releases, patch lifecycle, component skew, API groups, schemas, feature states, deprecations, downloads, and upgrade compatibility.

stimulus.title: K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An etcd snapshot alone restores every application volume, external dependency, credential, and user-visible service.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab K8s Disaster Recovery Capstone
steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend control-plane and application backups, etcd snapshots, encryption keys, manifests, external state, restore order, DNS and traffic cutover, recovery objectives, drills, and residual risk.
